prompt: The Compactness Theorem for first-order logic says:
options: If every FINITE subset of a set of sentences has a model, then the whole set has a model
Every consistent set of sentences is finite
Validity can be checked in finite time
hint: It lets you pass from 'every finite piece is satisfiable' to 'the whole infinite set is satisfiable'.
explanation: Compactness: a (possibly infinite) set of first-order sentences has a model if and only if every finite subset has a model. It's a cornerstone of model theory — used, for example, to build non-standard models of arithmetic by adding 'n is larger than every numeral' for all n.
